project-goal.title: Plan the map before you type
project-goal.bodyMarkdown:
A good terminal project stays readable because each folder name matches the job it does. In this project, you are building a small field kit map with clear checkpoints.

Worked example:
```bash
mkdir field-kit
cd field-kit
mkdir north south logs
```

Line by line: the first command creates the main project folder. The second command moves into it. The third command creates the three project subfolders inside that location.

project-workflow.title: Build the map in small command steps
project-workflow.bodyMarkdown:
Small steps are easier to check than one giant command sequence.

Worked example:
```bash
cd field-kit/north
pwd
ls
```

Step by step: the first command moves into the `north` folder. The second command confirms your new path. The third command shows what is inside that folder.

project-checking.title: Check the handoff before you leave
project-checking.bodyMarkdown: Before you click Check Answer, run one last `pwd` or `ls` to confirm that each note ended up in the folder where another person would expect to find it.
